acceptance_criteria: A cargo test calling lookup_symbols with name='Foo' against a mock HTTP server returns a typed SymbolLookupResponse with matches populated.
A cargo test calling lookup_symbols with no name/prefix/path raises a compile-time or runtime error before sending an HTTP request (not a backend 400 surprise).
A cargo test calling file_lens with a path containing '/' URL-encodes the path segment correctly (the outgoing request URL contains %2F not bare /).
A cargo test calling symbol_references against a mock returning callers and callees returns SymbolReferencesResponse with both lists populated.

StalenessState struct correctly deserializes both the 'fresh' shape ({ state: 'fresh', fresh: true, indexed_content_hash, indexed_parser_version }) and the 'unindexed' shape ({ state: 'unindexed', fresh: false, detail }).
All public async functions have a one-line doc comment citing the backend route.
nav_receipt_event_id and quiet_background_work_receipt_id are present in every response struct and are preserved (not dropped) through deserialization.
proof_targets: cargo build -p handshake-native 2>&1 | tail -5  ->  no errors
cargo test -p handshake-native knowledge_code_nav 2>&1  ->  all tests pass
grep -n '%2F\|percent_encode\|encode_path' src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/knowledge_code_nav.rs  ->  path encoding present for file_lens route
implementation_notes: The file_lens route path segment requires explicit %2F encoding: the backend decodes it with replace('%2F', '/') so a bare / in the path segment would be mis-parsed by axum's path extractor into multiple segments. Use url::form_urlencoded or manual replace when building the URL.
Symbol entity_id values are opaque strings (format: KE-<uuid>); do not parse them.
The LIST_CAP on the backend is 500; enforce limit <= 500 client-side (clamp, not error) to avoid over-fetching.

Each nav request leaves a KnowledgeRetrievalTraceRecorded EventLedger receipt on the backend (spec 2.3.13.11). The client exposes this receipt ID in the response struct for traceability; consumers should log or store it for audit.
The quiet_background_work_receipt_id comes from the ParallelSwarmStateRecoveryStore; it is present on all six routes. Preserve it even if your immediate consumer does not use it.
Do not fabricate actor_ids or session_ids: call sites must supply them from the current editor session context. Use EditorSessionContext or a similar type passed as a parameter.
The staleness contract: anything not provably fresh (fresh=true) is stale. Never render an unverified symbol as authoritative in the UI.

VERIFY vs MT-034 CodeNavClient + REUSE HEADERS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23): MT-034 (code<->note) ALREADY built a CodeNavClient in backend_client.rs that binds GET code symbols (resolve_code_ref / lookup_symbols / references). VERIFY whether MT-034's routes are the SAME API as this MT's /knowledge/code/* (MT-034 may have used a /code/symbols path vs this contract's /knowledge/code/symbols - confirm against api/knowledge_code_nav.rs which prefix is real). If SAME: REUSE/extend the existing CodeNavClient (add only the missing routes: tests, spans, file_lens) - do NOT fork a second symbol-lookup with divergent logic. If DISTINCT: bind fresh in backend/knowledge_code_nav.rs but reuse the same patterns. HEADERS: reuse MT-037's HskDocumentHeaders (same 5-field x-hsk-* contract) - import it / consolidate into a shared type, do NOT define a 4th copy of the header struct. The async-fn transport mirrors MT-037/038.

VERIFY 6 ROUTES + file_lens %2F + GUARDS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): VERIFY read-only against src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_code_nav.rs that the 6 routes + the response shapes (SymbolNavProjection, StalenessState, SymbolReferencesResponse callers/callees, FileLensResponse, the receipt fields) match. file_lens: the :path segment MUST %2F-encode embedded '/' (the backend decodes %2F->/; a bare / mis-parses in axum's path extractor -> 404, RISK-1/MC-1); test the OUTGOING URL contains %2F not bare /; REJECT absolute paths + any '..' segment from callers (path-traversal guard - security). lookup_symbols: pre-flight GUARD that >=1 of name/prefix/path is non-empty BEFORE building the request (RISK-2/MC-2 - typed LookupRequest or a check, not a backend-400 surprise). limit clamped <=500 client-side (RISK-6/MC-5). entity_id is an opaque string (KE-<uuid>) - do NOT parse/assume its format (RISK-5).

STALENESS TOLERANCE + RECEIPTS + FIXTURES (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - Spec-Realism): StalenessState.state is a TOLERANT String (NOT a closed enum) with #[serde(default)] on optional fields - it MUST deserialize an unknown future state ({state:'custom_future_state',fresh:false}) WITHOUT error/panic (RISK-3/MC-3/MC-6); the freshness rule = anything not fresh==true is stale, never render unverified as authoritative. PRESERVE nav_receipt_event_id + quiet_background_work_receipt_id in EVERY response struct (#[allow(dead_code)] if unused now - audit-trail requirement, RISK-4/MC-4). Mock-server fixture JSON MUST be REAL backend shapes (captured/field-verified vs api/knowledge_code_nav.rs handler json! blocks, cite lines - NOT self-serialized tautology). FULLY provable WITHOUT live PG (mock server) - no NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF. Stateless adapter (actor/session from a passed EditorSessionContext, never fabricated). NO GUI this MT (no screenshot/accesskit - pure client; document the AccessKit requirement for the future CodeSymbolPanel widget in the module doc). Full suite green; clippy clean; no tracked artifacts; base_url from config (portability).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.tsx - the full React component being ported; shows how symbol_entity_id drives getCodeSymbol, then conditionally loads getCodeFileLens using the staleness.indexed_content_hash and indexed_parser_version fields from the symbol response.
app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.test.tsx - test cases for the symbol panel; provides acceptance-criterion hints for the native widget (to be built in a later MT).

app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts - shows how Monaco integrates lookupCodeSymbols (lookup_symbols route), getCodeFileLens (file_lens route), getCodeSymbolReferences (symbol_references route). Functions configureHandshakeCodeIntelligence and codeSymbolStalenessLabel are reference patterns for the native egui code-lens equivalent.
app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.test.ts - reference tests for staleness label formatting.
app/src/harness/mt249_code_intelligence_harness.tsx - visual harness for the code intelligence integration; reference for what the native pane must display.

risks: file_lens route: bare '/' in path segment mis-parsed by axum into multiple path parts, causing 404. Client MUST percent-encode '/' as %2F when the path contains subdirectories.
lookup_symbols with no name/prefix/path returns backend 400; caller may silently get an error instead of a compilation guard.
StalenessState deserialized to the wrong variant when backend adds new state strings; must not panic on unknown states.
nav_receipt_event_id dropped by a consumer who only reads the payload causes audit trail gap (spec 2.3.13.11 requirement).
Symbol entity_id mis-parsed by client code assuming a specific prefix format causes lookup failures when the format changes.
Over-fetching with limit > 500 causes backend to clamp silently; client may think it got the full result set when it did not.
Stale symbol rendered as authoritative because freshness check is skipped in a fast-path causes user to act on outdated code navigation.
minimum_controls: file_lens path encoding enforced in the client and verified by a cargo test that inspects the outgoing request URL.
LookupRequest type or pre-flight guard ensures at least one of name/prefix/path is non-empty before building the HTTP request.
StalenessState uses #[serde(default)] on optional fields and does not panic on unknown state strings.
nav_receipt_event_id and quiet_background_work_receipt_id preserved in all response structs with #[allow(dead_code)] if not immediately used.
Cargo test validates that StalenessState { state: 'custom_future_state', fresh: false } deserializes without error.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-034 CodeNavClient + MT-037 HskDocumentHeaders + MT-037/038 binding pattern + Spec-Realism
corrections: verify vs MT-034 CodeNavClient (same /knowledge/code API? reuse/extend, add only missing tests/spans/file_lens; don't fork)
reuse MT-037 HskDocumentHeaders (no 4th copy); file_lens %2F-encode + reject absolute/.. paths; lookup pre-flight guard; limit<=500 clamp
StalenessState tolerant String (no panic on new states); preserve nav+quiet receipt ids in every struct
mock fixtures REAL backend shapes (no tautology); provable without live PG; stateless; no GUI; opaque entity_id

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_52c1591b (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: ead76049 feat MT-039 bind native editor to /knowledge/code/* nav API
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..9 + PT-1..4 PASS; 7 integration + 7 lib = 14 tests green; clippy clean; provable WITHOUT live PG (mock server); no GUI; no tracked artifacts.
implementation_result.steers_held: (1) VERIFY-OVER-PROSE: bound the REAL backend FileLens shape (CodeLensEntry/MonacoCodeLensPayload, monaco_bridge.rs:37-55/737) over the contract prose's different FileLensEntry fields. (2) reused MT-037 HskDocumentHeaders (nav header, system actor-kind). (3) file_lens encode_path_segment (% then / -> %2F) + unsafe-path (absolute/..) reject. (4) lookup EmptyLookup pre-flight guard before the wire. (5) StalenessState tolerant String (fresh/unindexed/custom_future_state all deserialize, no panic). (6) nav_receipt_event_id + quiet_background_work_receipt_id preserved in all 6 structs. (7) limit<=500 clamp; opaque entity_id. Mock fixtures REAL (vs handler json! blocks).
implementation_result.client_proliferation_note: There are now distinct code-nav clients by purpose: MT-008 code_editor::code_nav (Value-based inline graceful-degradation for the editor), MT-034 CodeNavClient (cross-ref resolve), MT-039 KnowledgeCodeNavClient (the typed backend/ consolidation). Panel reviewed + hardened (3 must-fixes) - distinct purposes/transports, not a pure fork. A future cleanup could unify them but each is currently justified.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: backend/knowledge_code_nav.rs + backend/mod.rs + backend/knowledge_documents.rs (header reuse) + backend_client.rs + test in allowed_paths.
